items.title: New in Java 14
items.summary: I hve updated the Java introduction to list what is new in Java 14 which is due for release in March 2020.

items.title: Java Stack
items.summary: The Java Stack tutorial has been updated to show examples with generic types, and mentioning the use of Deque as a more modern alternative to Stack.

items.title: Tutorials - New Layout
items.summary: We are currently experimenting with the layout of tutorials.jenkov.com, so if the layout looks a bit weird - please be patient. We will fix it soon. If not, let us know, and we will fix it.

items.title: Java System.in, System.out and System.err
items.summary: The Java System.in, System.out and System.err streams provide a simple way to send data into a Java application, and output data from a Java app too. This tutorial has been clarified.

items.title: RION Encoding
items.summary: The RION Encoding tutorial has been updated to be more clear, and to also contain encoding examples in hexadecimal notation, so you can now see exactly what RION encoded data looks like.

items.title: RION Design Goals
items.summary: RION was designed to be fast, compact, expressive, suitable as network protocol message format, as well as a few other goals. This text explains the design goals of RION.

items.title: RION Introduction
items.summary: RION is a fast, compact, versatile, binary data format. RION was designed for data exchange, but works fine as a data storage format too. The RION introduction has been extended and improved.

items.title: Jackson JsonNode Traversal
items.summary: The Jackson JsonNode tutorial has been extended to explain how to traverse a JsonNode graph representing either a JSON object or JSON Array.

items.title: Concurrency vs Parallelism
items.summary: Concurrency and parallelism are two commonly used term when talking about multithreading - but what do they mean exactly? This tutorial explains the difference. The explanations have been updated to be more clear.

items.title: Java File
items.summary: The Java File tutorial has been updated. The Java File class gives you access to file system operations like renaming or deleting files or directories, check if a file exists, list directory content etc.

items.title: Java DataInputStream
items.summary: The Java DataInputStream enables you to read primitive data types (byte, short int, float etc) from an underlying InputStream. The Java DataInputStream tutorial has been updated to explain more of its available read methods.

items.title: Same-threading
items.summary: Same-threading is single-threaded design scaled out to N threads. I have updated my same-threading tutorial to explain this more precisely.

items.title: Java RandomAccessFile
items.summary: The Java RandomAccessFile class enables you to read from and write to a file at arbitrary locations. In other words, you can move freely around the file, both forth and back, and read from or write to the file at the desired location. I have updated the Java RandomAccessFile tutorial to explain better how to use it.

items.title: Stream Processing API Designs
items.summary: Having studied various different stream processing APIs over the last 1,5 years, I have written up an analysis of what to look for when choosing a stream processing API.

items.title: Java ZipFile - Zip Slip Attack
items.summary: I have updated the Java ZipFile tutorial to explain how to avoid the Zip Slip attack !

items.title: JavaFX TableView
items.summary: The JavaFX TableView enables you to display data in a tabular control. The TableView tutorial has been updated to explain the TableView SelectionModel - how rows are selected in the TableView.

items.title: Concurrency Models
items.summary: Over time the developer community has come up with several different concurrency models, each with their own pros and cons. This concurrency model tutorial has been updated to explain the shared state and separate state variations of a concurrency model.

items.title: JavaFX Button
items.summary: The JavaFX Button represents a visual button the user can click to perform some action. The JavaFX Button tutorial has been updated with information about how to disable a button so it is visible but not clickable.

items.title: Java Concurrency Tutorial
items.summary: I have updated the Java Concurrency tutorial introduction to be more up-to-date. In general I will be updating the tutorials in this trail over the coming months.

items.title: Java ByteArrayOutputStream
items.summary: The Java ByteArrayOutputStream can collect the bytes written to and return them as a Java byte array. Thus, the Java ByteArrayOutputStream is handy when you have a component that can only write to an OutputStream, but you need the data as a byte array. This Java ByteArrayOutputStream tutorial has been updated with more descriptions and examples.

items.title: Java ByteArrayInputStream
items.summary: The Java ByteArrayInputStream can turn a Java byte array into an InputStream. Thus, the Java ByteArrayInputStream is very useful when you have a byte array, but need an InputStream for some purpose. This Java ByteArrayInputStream tutorial has been updated with more descriptions and examples.

items.title: Java ThreadLocal
items.summary: The Java ThreadLocal provides a variable where values set can only be read by the same thread that set them. The Java ThreadLocal tutorial has now been updated with more information about setting initial value, removing the value etc.

items.title: Java Collections Tutorial
items.summary: Over the course of the last year, most of the tutorials in the Java Collections tutorial series have been updated, and 3 new tutorials have been added.

items.title: Java NavigableMap
items.summary: The Java NavigableMap tutorial has been cleaned up with more descriptions and more examples.

items.title: Java BufferedReader
items.summary: The Java BufferedReader tutorial has been updated with information about how to implement your own reusable BufferedReader.

items.title: Java 13 Features
items.summary: The Java tutorial introduction has been updated with a list of the new features in Java 13.

items.title: Java BufferedInputStream
items.summary: The Java BufferedInputStream tutorial has been updated with an example of how to create a reusable BufferedInputStream.

items.title: Mem Ops Bytes
items.summary: The Mem Ops Bytes class represents a byte sequence allocated from a shared byte array. Bytes is designed for pooling and freeing of both itself and the allocated bytes.

items.title: Mem Ops 0.6.2
items.summary: Mem Ops 0.6.2 is out, which has more advanced pooling of Bytes objects which represents byte sequences allocated in a shared byte array.

items.title: RION Ops in Central Maven Repo
items.summary: RION Ops for Java- an open source toolkit for reading and writing the compact, fast binary RION data format, is now available in the Maven central repo.

items.title: Maven Java Compiler - Java 9 and Later
items.summary: The Maven Java Compiler tutorial has been updated to explain how to set the Java compiler for Java 9 and later.

items.title: Java SortedMap
items.summary: The Java SortedMap is like a Map where the keys are kept sorted internally, so the keys can be iterated later in sort order, or reverse sort order.

items.title: Mem Ops for Java Available in Maven Central Repo
items.summary: Mem Ops for Java, the open source toolkit for memory allocation in apps that require stable memory consumption under high load, is now available in the central Maven repository. The Mem Ops for Java tutorial now describes the Maven coordinates required to use Mem Ops with Maven.

items.title: Dockerfile Tutorial
items.summary: The Docker Dockerfile contains the recipe for how to build a Docker image. You can ask Docker to execute a Dockerfile and build a Docker image as a result.

items.title: Java NavigableSet
items.summary: The Java NavigableSet is a sub-interface of Java SortedSet, with a few extra navigation methods. The Java NavigableSet tutorial has been updated.

items.title: Docker Tutorial
items.summary: Docker containers are an alternative to virtual machines which have less overhead, and which can be created from a Dockerfile (configuration file), so you can recreate the container again and again.

items.title: Java PushbackInputStream
items.summary: The Java PushbackInputStream enables you to push back bytes into the PushbackInputStream which has been read from it - for later re-reading. This is useful when parsing binary data formats.

items.title: Java PushbackReader
items.summary: The Java PushbackReader enables you to push back characters into the PushbackReader which has been read from it - for later re-reading. This is useful when parsing text or textual data formats.

items.title: RION Ops RionReader
items.summary: The RION Ops RionReader is able to read RION fields, e.g. written with the RionWriter, into various Java data types.

items.title: RION Ops RionWriter
items.summary: The RION Ops RionWriter is able to write various types of data as RION fields into a destination byte array. These RION Fields can later be read using the RION Ops RionReader.

items.title: RION Ops
items.summary: RION Ops is an open source toolkit to work with the compact, fast, binary data format RION.

items.title: RION Data Format
items.summary: The RION data format is a binary, compact, fast general purpose data format. RION was formerly known as ION, but has been renamed to distinguish it from Amazon's ION data format.

items.title: Java SortedSet
items.summary: The Java SortedSet is a Set which keeps its element sorted internally, so they can be iterated in the sorted order.

items.title: Jackson ObjectMapper
items.summary: The Jackson ObjectMapper is one of the most central classes of the Jackson JSON parser / generator API. The Jackson ObjectMapper tutorial has been updated with information about how to convert from JsonNode to objects, and from objects to JsonNode instances.

items.title: Jackson JsonNode
items.summary: The Jackson JsonNode class is a dynamic representation of a JSON object. The JsonNode tutorial has received a major round of updates.

items.title: Build a Fat JAR With Maven
items.summary: You can configure Maven to build a Fat JAR from your project containing all your project's compiled classes, and all classes of all dependencies your project uses, in single JAR file called a Fat JAR. Very useful for packaging microservices, executable JARs etc. - also inside a Docker container.

items.title: Java InputStreamReader
items.summary: The Java InputStreamReader tutorial has received a major round of updates. The InputStreamReader turns a byte based InputStream into a character based Reader.

items.title: Java InputStream
items.summary: The Java InputStream tutorial has received a major round of updates. InputStream represents an inbound stream of bytes.

items.title: Java IO Tutorial
items.summary: The Java IO Tutorial has been updated in several places. Many of the core texts have been updated and some almost completely rewritten.

items.title: Jackson JsonNode
items.summary: The Jackson JsonNode tutorial has been updated with a description about how to access fields using a JSON path expression (like XPath) via the at() method.

items.title: Java Properties Tutorial
items.summary: The Java Properties tutorial has received a major round of updates, covering more advanced topics of the Java Properties class, like default properties, loading from classpath etc.

items.title: The Store and Process Stream Processing Design Pattern
items.summary: The store and process stream processing design pattern is a simple design which is easy to implement and which can scale nicely, both in terms of complexity of stream processing logic and performance.

items.title: JavaFX HTMLEditor
items.summary: The JavaFX HTMLEditor enables the user to edit HTML in a wysiwyg editor.

items.title: JavaFX Pagination
items.summary: The JavaFX Pagination control enables the user to navigate page by page through content, e.g. search results, articles, images etc.

items.title: JavaFX ColorPicker
items.summary: A JavaFX ColorPicker enables the user to choose a color from a small wizard-like popup dialog.

items.title: JavaFX Separator
items.summary: The JavaFX Separator shows a visual divider between JavaFX controls inside a container, e.g. inside a VBox or HBox.

items.title: JavaFX ContextMenu
items.summary: A JavaFX ContextMenu provides a right click menu which can be attached to a JavaFX control. This JavaFX ContextMenu tutorial explains how to use it.

items.title: Stream Ops StreamOps Facade Class
items.summary: The StreamOps class in the Stream Ops data streaming API is a convenience facade class that can act as a starting point when using Stream Ops.

items.title: JavaFX SplitMenuButton
items.summary: The JavaFX SplitMenuButton is an advanced MenuButton which can have an extra, often used menu choice available to click on directly, as a side-mounted button.

items.title: JavaFX ScrollPane
items.summary: A JavaFX ScrollPane is a container that has two scrollbars around the component it contains if the component is larger than the visible area of the ScrollPane. The scrollbars enable the user to scroll around the component shown inside the ScrollPane, so different parts of the component can be seen.
